title: 10 best herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream contains antioxidants like lutein and zeaxanthin, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, promoting healing and soothing dry, cracked heels.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: The cream's emollient ingredients, such as fatty acids and triglycerides, provide long-lasting hydration to the skin, filling in cracks and crevices, and helping to soften and smooth rough patches.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to cracked heels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with cracked heels because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpene saponins and carotenoids, help to soothe and calm dry, cracked skin. Calendula's natural moisturizing properties lock in hydration, promoting skin elasticity and reducing the appearance of cracks. Regular application of Calendula cream can also help to reduce the risk of further irritation and infection, promoting healthy, smooth skin on the heels.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help to soothe and protect cracked heels by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Calendula, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels.

Naphthoquinones (specifically, calanol): Calanol, a naphthoquinone present in Calendula, exhibits antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections and promote a healthy environment for wound healing on cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for cracked heels due to their high content of carotenoids and flavonoids, which help to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: They are used to extract calenduloside, a triterpene saponin with anti-inflammatory properties, which can be incorporated into creams for cracked heels to provide soothing relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula flowers in full sun at peak bloom for optimal potency and freshness in large quantities.
Infuse 2 cups of the calendula flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of squeezing.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and cools to a consistent creamy texture in approximately 20 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Tea tree oil has potent antibacterial and antifungal compounds that help to prevent infections and promote healing in cracked heels.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cracked heels, allowing for a more comfortable healing process.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: Tea tree oil's emollient and moisturizing properties help to soften and hydrate dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing the appearance of cracks.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with cracked heels because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea tree oil present in the cream effectively combats the fungal and bacterial infections that cause cracked heels. It also promotes wound healing, reduces inflammation, and moisturizes the skin, making it an ideal treatment for dry, cracked heels. Regular application of tea tree cream helps to repair and soften the skin, leaving the feet feeling smooth and healthy.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of cracked heels by creating an environment that prevents bacterial and fungal infections.
Linalool: As an antiseptic and antifungal compound, Linalool aids in the treatment of cracked heels by preventing the growth of pathogens that can worsen the condition.
Catechin: This phenolic compound exhibits antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote the regeneration of healthy skin cells to repair cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for cracked heels due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin.
Seeds: They are used to add moisturizing properties to creams for cracked heels, as they contain a high amount of oil that can help to hydrate and soften dry skin.

Barks: They are used to make creams for cracked heels due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect the skin from further damage.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and place them in a heat-resistant container.
Mix 100 milliliters of oil such as sweet almond oil with 50 milliliters of distilled water.
Combine the oil and water mixture with the dried leaves in the heat-resistant container and heat it.
Infuse the mixture for 2 hours at 60 degrees celsius then let it cool completely.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and add 10 grams of beeswax.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cracked heels.
Antimicrobial: Chamomile's antimicrobial property prevents bacterial and fungal infections from spreading in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing risk of complications.
Soilant and soothing: Chamomile's gentle, non-irritating properties help moisturize and soothe dry, cracked skin, promoting skin elasticity and flexibility.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with cracked heels because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile oil reduce redness and irritation, calming the skin and promoting healing. Additionally, chamomile's emollient properties lock in moisture, softening and hydrating the dry cracked skin. By regularly applying a chamomile cream, you can help to repair and protect your heels, leaving them feeling smooth and healthy. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from cracked heels.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that helps with cracked heels by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Alpha-bisabolol: Alpha-bisabolol is a sesquiterpene alcohol that helps with cracked heels by providing a soothing and moisturizing effect, reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration.

Matricine: Matricine is a sesquiterpene glycoside that helps with cracked heels by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting the repair of damaged skin tissue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make creams for cracked heels because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and soften the skin.
Leaves: Used to make creams for cracked heels because of their emollient and moisturizing properties, which help to lock in moisture and promote skin elasticity.
Seeds: Used to make creams for cracked heels because of their rich content of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which help to nourish and protect the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at the peak of their potency to ensure maximum efficacy.
Infuse 2 cups of distilled water with 1 cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 to 15 minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and whip until light and fluffy for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by dry skin, promoting healing and protecting against further damage.

Wound-healing properties: The flavonoids and hypericin in St John's Wort have been shown to accelerate wound closure and improve the strength of the skin, making it an effective treatment for cracked heels.
Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with cracked heels, creating a more comfortable healing environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with cracked heels because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in the herb, such as hyperforin and hypericin, promote the healing process by reducing inflammation and preventing infection in the affected area. The cream also helps to lock in moisture and promote the growth of new skin cells, leading to a faster recovery and a softer, smoother texture of the skin on the heels. This results in a significant reduction of discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery of cracked heels.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid and powerful antioxidant, quercetin can help soothe and protect the skin from further damage, promoting the healing of cracked heels.
Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infection and promote the healing process of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, leaves help to soothe and protect cracked heels.
Stems: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, stems aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other bioactive compounds, flowers help to accelerate tissue repair and regeneration in cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Extract 25g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in a glass container using 500ml of glycerin and 250ml of distilled water.
Infuse the mixture for 2 hours at room temperature or 30 minutes in a double boiler.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and collect the liquid extract.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 10g of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Combine the herbal extract with the beeswax mixture and stir until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English lavender has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to combat infections and prevent further infection of cracked heels, promoting healthy healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels, making them more comfortable and easier to manage.
Moisturizing properties: The fatty acids and vitamins in English lavender cream help to lock in moisture, soften dry skin, and promote the natural healing process of cracked heels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with cracked heels because of its exceptional moisturizing and soothing properties. The cream's high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils works to deeply hydrate and repair dry, cracked skin. As the cream is absorbed, it helps to reduce inflammation and promote the regeneration of healthy skin cells. Regular use can lead to softer, smoother heels that are less prone to cracking and splitting. This makes English lavender creams an effective and natural solution for cracked heel relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to moisturize and soften the skin, reducing dryness and cracks in the heels.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with cracked heels, and promoting healing.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting collagen synthesis, which helps to improve skin texture and reduce the appearance of cracks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and heal cracked skin.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used for their calming and moisturizing effects, which can help to soften and protect dry, cracked heels.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain essential oils that can help to reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration, making them beneficial for cracked heel creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning hours for optimal potency and flavor.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the dried flowers.
Whip 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of the infused lavandula angustifolia oil until smooth and creamy.
Pour the whipped mixture into small glass jars and let it set at room temperature for about 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal wheat creams containing anti-inflammatory herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, or lavender can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cracked heels, allowing for faster healing.

Moisturizing properties: Ingredients like shea butter, coconut oil, or olive oil in herbal wheat creams provide long-lasting moisture to dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing the appearance of cracks.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbs like neem, calendula, or eucalyptus in herbal wheat creams have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help prevent infection and promote wound healing by eliminating bacteria and fungi that can aggravate cracked heels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Triticum aestivum, also known as wheat, creams helps with cracked heels because of its rich nutrient content and moisturizing properties. The cream's active ingredients, derived from the wheat plant, work to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin. The emollient properties of wheat cream help to soften and smooth the skin, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures. Regular application can also promote healthy skin regeneration, leaving heels feeling soft and supple. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief from dry, cracked heels.
remedy_constituents: Fiber: High in soluble fiber, Triticum aestivum creams may help to lock in moisture and reduce dryness of the skin, thereby soothing and protecting cracked heels.

Avenanthramides: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels, promoting faster healing and recovery.

Saponins: Saponins in Triticum aestivum creams may help to clean and exfoliate the skin, removing dead skin cells and promoting cell turnover, which can help to improve the texture and appearance of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Wheat seeds are used due to their high content of emollients and fatty acids that help to moisturize and soften dry skin.
Stems: Wheat stem extracts are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect cracked heels.
Leaves: Wheat leaf extracts are used for their antioxidant and moisturizing properties, which help to nourish and repair dry skin on the heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh triticum aestivum roots and leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested triticum aestivum roots and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Steep 1/2 cup of dried triticum aestivum roots and leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and reserve the oil.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter and melt the mixture.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to reduce moisture and shrink the appearance of pores, which can aid in drying out and reducing the size of cracked heels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch Hazel has anti-inflammatory compounds like tannins and flavonoids that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with cracked heels.

Antimicrobial properties: Witch Hazel's antimicrobial agents, such as gallic acid and salicin, have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing in cracked heels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American Witch Hazel, creams helps with cracked heels because of its exceptional astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream helps to dry out the cracked skin, reducing the moisture that can exacerbate the condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of Witch Hazel also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. As a result, the cream promotes healing and helps to repair the cracked skin, leaving it smoother and more moisturized. This makes it an effective solution for cracked heels.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help with cracked heels by providing a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing moisture loss and inflammation, and promoting a healing environment.
Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin contributes to the treatment of cracked heels by exhibiting antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing bacterial growth and soothing irritated skin.

Astringent Phenolics: Astringent phenolics, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, help with cracked heels by reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and tightening the skin to reduce moisture loss and prevent further irritation.
remedy_parts: Barks: Hamamelis virginiana barks are commonly used due to their high tannin content, which provides astringent and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and protect cracked heels.
Leaves: Hamamelis virginiana leaves are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing of cracked skin on the heels.
Rhyzomes: Hamamelis virginiana rhyzomes contain a high concentration of hamamelitannin, a compound that provides antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to protect and heal cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried hamamelis virginiana flowers and leaves to 3 parts of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily for 1 minute.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Mix 1 part of the infused hamamelis virginiana oil with 1 part of beeswax and 1 part of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for about 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax and cocoa butter have melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels, promoting a more comfortable healing process.
Moisturizing properties: The emollient and humectant properties of dandelion cream help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures on the heel.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking on the heels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with cracked heels because of its potent anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as inulin and taraxasterol, penetrate deep into the skin to soothe and soften dry, cracked areas. By locking in moisture and reducing inflammation, Taraxacum officinale cream accelerates the healing process and promotes the growth of healthy skin cells, leaving cracked heels smooth and hydrated. Regular use can also help prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin helps retain moisture in the skin, reducing dryness and cracking.
Taraxasterol: A triterpene saponin, taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and protect the skin from further damage.
Phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic acid, ferulic acid): These antioxidants may help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, contributing to the repair of cracked skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams for cracked heels due to their high content of inulin, which helps to soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to create creams for cracked heels due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm irritated skin.
Seeds: The seeds of Taraxacum officinale are used to make creams for cracked heels because they contain essential fatty acids, which help to nourish and moisturize dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers when they are fresh and in full bloom approximately 1 cup of dried taraxacum officinale.
Dry the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers using a low heat dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Infuse 1/2 cup of dried taraxacum officinale in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to a consistency suitable for application to the skin in about 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cracked heels, allowing for faster healing and relief.

Antibacterial and antifungal property: Plantain creams have antibacterial and antifungal properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy wound healing.

Moisturizing and emollient property: Plantain creams are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as fatty acids, which help lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with cracked heels because it possesses anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The plant's extract has been traditionally used to soothe and soften dry skin, making it an effective remedy for cracked heels. The cream helps to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of cracks and promoting healthy skin regeneration. Regular application of plantain cream can lead to smoother, more hydrated skin, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with cracked heels.
remedy_constituents: Apolipids: These lipids help to moisturize and protect the skin, reducing dryness and cracking associated with cracked heels.

Triterpenoids (specifically, aucubin and acubin): As anti-inflammatory compounds, these triterpenoids help to soothe and reduce inflammation in the skin, promoting healing and reducing pain associated with cracked heels.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These antioxidants help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting collagen production and improving skin elasticity, which can help to reduce the appearance of cracks and fissures in the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Seeds: Used because they contain a high amount of plant sterols, which can help to protect and repair cracked skin.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in healing and preventing infection of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried plantago major leaves and flowers to ensure their potency and freshness.
Combine the dried herbs with 500 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours and 30 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove any solids.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter into the oil to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream reduces inflammation and swelling associated with cracked heels, promoting healing and soothing discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties in stinging nettle cream help to prevent infection and promote the growth of healthy skin by controlling the spread of harmful bacteria and fungi.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Stinging nettle cream's moisturizing and emollient properties help to lock in moisture, soften and hydrate dry, cracked skin, and reduce roughness and scaling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with cracked heels because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's high concentration of vitamins A and E, as well as its natural humectant properties, helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin. Additionally, Urtica dioica's ability to stimulate blood flow and promote collagen production helps to accelerate the healing process, leaving heels feeling softer, smoother, and more resilient. This makes it an effective natural solution for cracked heels.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote healing in cracked heels by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections.
Triterpenoids (specifically, ursolic acid): This constituent helps to reduce inflammation and improve skin integrity by reducing oxidative stress and enhancing the skin's natural barrier function.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid): This compound helps to reduce inflammation and improve the skin's ability to retain moisture, thus promoting the healing of cracked heels and preventing further damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of urtic acid, which provides anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Stems: Stems are also utilized for their content of urtic acid and other compounds that help soothe and moisturize dry, cracked skin.
Roots: Roots are used for their potential to stimulate collagen production and improve skin elasticity, making them beneficial for cracked heel treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam the harvested urtica dioica for 20 minutes to reduce the urushiol content and increase the bioavailability.
Mix the steamed urtica dioica with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and then let it infuse for 2 hours to allow the herbal properties to combine.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before filling the herbal cream into a jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients that provide intense moisturization and nourishment. The cream's rich antioxidants and emollients penetrate deep into the skin, repairing damaged tissue and hydrating dry, rough patches. Additionally, the Tasmanian blue gum extract itself has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritation and discomfort, promoting healing and flexibility in even the most stubborn cases of cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oils present in the cream, such as eucalyptol and globulol, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and worsen cracked heel symptoms.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's natural waxes and oils help to lock in moisture, soothe dry skin, and provide a protective barrier against further drying and cracking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated skin on cracked heels.
Barks: Containing natural astringents, barks aid in tightening and firming up dry, cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground eucalyptus powder with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a makeshift one created by a stainless steel bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Stir occasionally for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix well, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens into a smooth cream, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry creams helps with cracked heels because of its rich emollient properties that deeply moisturize and nourish dry, rough skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and other plant extracts, work together to soften and soothe cracked heels, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy cell growth. As a result, the skin becomes smoother, softer, and more resilient, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Barberry creams contain berberine, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cracked heels, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can exacerbate cracked heels, promoting healthy skin regeneration and preventing further damage.

Exfoliating and moisturizing properties: Barberry creams contain emollients and humectants that gently exfoliate dead skin cells, while also moisturizing and hydrating the skin, helping to soften and soothe dry, cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soften and moisturize dry skin, making them effective for treating cracked heels.
Buds: Containing high amounts of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh berberis vulgaris leaves and stems in the early morning dew.
Dry the harvested plant material for at least two hours under shade or in a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for one hour.
Grind 100 grams of dried berberis vulgaris into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground berberis vulgaris with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and let it cool before using as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique combination of soothing and moisturizing properties. The licorice root extract in the cream has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels. Additionally, the cream's emollient ingredients deeply nourish and hydrate the skin, filling in cracks and crevices to provide long-lasting relief from discomfort and dryness. As a result, herbal licorice creams offer effective and natural treatment for dry, cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin by reducing inflammation and promoting hydration, making it ideal for relieving dryness and cracking on the feet.
Licoric acid: Licoric acid has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to prevent infections and reduce the risk of further irritation or damage to the cracked heel area.
Saponins: Saponins have emollient and moisturizing properties that help to soften and hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and cracking, and promoting healthy skin cell turnover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help relieve dryness and itching associated with cracked heels.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that exhibit moisturizing and protective effects, making them useful for treating dry and damaged skin on the feet.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 250g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water and stir until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60-70°c.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it steep for 30 minutes to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discard the solids, and let the cream cool before refrigerating it for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique blend of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. The cream's active ingredients, including allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to promote skin regeneration and improve tissue elasticity. This helps to soften and repair dry, cracked skin on the heels, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy, smooth appearance. As a result, comfrey creams provide effective relief for individuals suffering from dry, cracked heels, leaving their skin feeling soft, supple, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey creams contain allantoin, a natural humectant that helps to lock in moisture and promote skin regeneration, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures on the heel.
Tannins: The tannins present in comfrey cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels, making it easier for the skin to heal.
Allyl sulfides: Comfrey creams contain allyl sulfides, which have antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote a healthy healing environment for the skin on the heel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain a high concentration of allantoin, a natural compound that promotes skin regeneration and helps to repair dry, cracked heels.
Buds: Comfrey buds are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated skin, making them ideal for soothing dry, cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 30 minutes to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine 50g of dried symphytum officinale leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 2 weeks, shaking daily, to allow for infusion.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth and combine it with 200g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter to create a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet creams helps with cracked heels because of its exceptional moisturizing properties. The cream's rich blend of natural oils, including coconut oil and shea butter, deeply hydrates and nourishes dry, cracked skin on the heels. Additionally, sweet violet extract has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities that help soothe irritation, reduce redness, and promote healing. As a result, the cream provides long-lasting relief from discomfort and promotes healthy, soft, and supple skin on the heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet violet's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels.
Moisturizing properties: The emollient and humectant properties of sweet violet cream help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy hydration and barrier function.

Antimicrobial properties: Sweet violet's antimicrobial compounds, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, help to prevent infection and promote wound healing by controlling the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming dry, cracked skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide moisture to dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest viola odorata leaves and flowers and dry them properly in shade for 7-10 days.
Mix dried herbs with beeswax pellets, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture until beeswax melts completely and blend well to create a smooth consistency.
Add vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes before pouring into jars.
Allow the cream to cool down, set, and mature for at least 24 hours before applying on cracked heels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with cracked heels because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's natural ingredients, such as lemon balm extract and glycerin, work together to calm and moisturize dry, cracked skin. As a result, the cream helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, filling in cracks and crevices to leave feet feeling soft, smooth, and nourished. Additionally, the cream's antioxidants help to protect the skin from further damage, promoting overall foot health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial compounds help to prevent the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can exacerbate cracked heels and cause infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels, promoting faster healing and soothing discomfort.

Moisturizing properties: Lemon balm's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, soften and hydrate dry skin, and promote the formation of a protective barrier that shields the skin from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and treating dry, cracked skin.
Buds: Lemon balm buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, making them suitable for creams aimed at repairing damaged skin on heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dark place for seven days to reduce moisture.
Combine one part dried melissa officinalis with four parts sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely incorporating all ingredients smoothly.
Strain the cooled cream through cheesecloth into sterilized jars for long shelf life storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique ability to deeply moisturize and nourish dry, rough skin. The herbal extract in cleavers cream works wonders by locking in moisture, reducing inflammation, and promoting cell regeneration. This results in a significant reduction in the appearance of cracks and fissures, leaving heels soft, smooth, and supple. Regular use of cleavers creams can also help to prevent future cracking by maintaining healthy skin hydration levels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers cream reduces inflammation and swelling in cracked heels, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Cleavers cream's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and slow down the healing process, keeping cracked heels soft and supple.

Wound-healing properties: Cleavers cream contains compounds that stimulate collagen production, improve blood circulation, and promote cellular regeneration, helping to accelerate the repair of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soften and moisturize dry skin, making them effective for soothing cracked heels.
Barks: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain associated with cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried galium aparine into a fine powder using an electric grinder.
Mix the powdered galium aparine with 250ml of coconut oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer to desired consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cracked heels are topical treatments infused with natural ingredients that help to moisturize, soften, and heal dry, cracked skin on the heels. These creams typically contain herbs such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and shea butter, which work together to soothe and repair damaged skin. Examples of herbal creams include coconut oil and beeswax-based creams, which can improve lives by providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and pain, allowing individuals to engage in daily activities with confidence and comfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cracked heels that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and dry skin conditions. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and coconut oil help to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These creams can also be used to treat conditions such as keratosis pilaris, which causes rough patches on the arms and legs.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with cracked heels is a blend of aloe vera, shea butter, and tea tree oil. Aloe vera moisturizes and soothes dry skin, while shea butter deeply nourishes and protects cracked heels. Tea tree oil, with its antimicrobial properties, helps to prevent infections and promote healing. Applying a mixture of these creams, ideally after exfoliating and before bedtime, can help to repair and soften cracked heels over time, promoting healthy and smooth skin.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Wounds : a compendium of clinical research and practice, herbal creams can be beneficial for cracked heels due to their wound healing properties. The study, which reviewed 14 clinical studies on diabetic foot ulcers, found that herbal medicines, including those administered as topical creams, can improve parameters related to wound healing such as ulcer width and depth, phagocytic function, and epithelialization. By applying herbal creams, individuals with cracked heels may experience accelerated wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved skin regeneration, ultimately leading to healthier and more moisturized skin.
